# Reproducible minimal FFmpeg runtime

The release runtime is built from these immutable upstream revisions:

- FFmpeg commit: `a09be9b91e8e1219f297586873b0d7322b47df96`
- nv-codec-headers commit: `15ee32753c92faddbabbff11676779618fc6db7e`

The Dockerfile cross-compiles Windows x64 shared libraries using only FFmpeg's
built-in LGPL code and the MIT-licensed nv-codec-headers. It deliberately uses
`--disable-autodetect` and does not enable GPL, nonfree, libx264, libx265, or
other optional third-party codec libraries.

Build from the repository root:

```powershell
.\build-minimal-ffmpeg.ps1
```

Outputs are written below `artifacts/ffmpeg-minimal/`:

- `runtime/`: redistributable DLLs and license texts
- `development/`: headers and import libraries used to compile the backend
- `sources/`: exact corresponding source archives and the complete configure log

The complete build recipe is this Dockerfile. The source archives generated by
the script must be uploaded beside every release that distributes these DLLs.
Do not replace the pinned revisions with a floating `latest` download.

FFmpeg remains licensed by its contributors under LGPL-2.1-or-later. The
surrounding application is a separate work and dynamically links to unmodified
FFmpeg DLLs. Users may replace those DLLs with ABI-compatible modified builds.

Upstream projects:

- https://ffmpeg.org/
- https://github.com/FFmpeg/FFmpeg
- https://github.com/FFmpeg/nv-codec-headers
